heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 225 on 28 May 2026, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 30 June 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwave in progress.  So far, 2 heatwaves have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 28 June 2026, with only 68 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (220°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 3360 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 5937 lux) and cloud levels (up 805 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 222.7m lower than yesterday, 804.6m lower than the past week, 12.7m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1285m minimum and 4200m maximum, with an average of 2300m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 3100m and 2280m.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; warm but comfortable temperatures are expected. Also, the air will be thick and uncomfortable and little or no wind. Possible light showers, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and sunlight will be very intense and bright. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
